UiString.ConfigWindow_ConditionSetDesc: The condition value you chose. Click to modify.
UiString.ConfigWindow_NoRotation: No rotations loaded! Please check the rotations tab!
UiString.ConfigWindow_About_Punchline: Analyzes PvE combat information in every frame and finds the best action.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Rotation_InvalidRo:
Invalid Rotation! 
Please update to the latest version or contact {0}!
UiString.ConfigWindow_About_Description:
This includes almost all information available in one combat frame, including the status of all party members, hostile target statuses, skill cooldowns, MP and HP of characters, character locations, hostile target casting status, combo state, combat duration, player level, etc.

It will then highlight the best action on the hotbar, or help you click it.
UiString.ConfigWindow_About_Warning:
This is designed for GENERAL COMBAT, not for Savage or Ultimate content. 

Use it carefully! While not designed specifically for Savage or Ultimate content RSR works fine in them, but it will not solve mechanics for you. Pay attention and use macros.
UiString.ConfigWindow_About_ClickingCoun: RSR has helped you by clicking actions {0:N0} times.
UiString.ConfigWindow_About_Compatibilit:
Rotation Solver helps you choose targets and click actions. Any plugin that changes these will affect its decisions.

Here is a list of plugins that have historically (but not always) caused compatibility issues:
Cannot properly execute the behavior that RSR wants to perform.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_Descriptio: Used to customize when RSR uses specific actions automatically. Click on an action's icon in the left list. Below, you may set the conditions for when that specific action is used. Each action can have different conditions to override the default rotation behavior.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_IsIntercep: Allow action to be intercepted by the intercept system
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_IsRestrict: Prevent this action against a curated list of mobs (ie. Jagd Dolls)
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_MinHPFeatu: Allow action to be restricted by the minimum HP feature
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_MinHPPerce: If target is below this percent, do not use this action
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_SkipPositi: Skip BossModReborn position-safety check for this movement action
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_TTK: Time-to-kill threshold required for this action to be used
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_AoeCount: Number of targets needed to use this action
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_CheckStatu: Should this action check needed status effects
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_CheckTarge: Should this action check targets needed status effects
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_GcdCount: Number of GCDs before the DOT/Status effect is reapplied
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_HealRatio: HP ratio for automatic healing (only applies to healing actions)
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_ConditionD: Forced Conditions have higher priority. If Forced Conditions are met, Disabled Conditions will be ignored.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_ForcedCond: Conditions for forced automatic use of action
UiString.ConfigWindow_Actions_DisabledCo: Conditions that disable automatic use of an action
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_Description: In this window, you can set parameters that can be customized using lists.
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_Invincibility: Ignores target if it has one of these statuses
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_PriorityDesc: Attacks the target first if it has one of these statuses
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_NoCastingStat: Do not take action if you have one of these debuffs
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_HostileCastin: Use tank personal damage mitigation abilities if the target is casting any of these actions
Use AoE damage mitigation abilities if the target is casting any of these actions
Use knockback prevention abilities if the target is casting any of these actions
Stop casting or taking actions if the enemy is casting this ability
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_NoHostileDesc: Enemies that will never be targeted
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_NoHostilesNam: The name of the enemy that you don't want to target
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_NoProvokeDesc: Enemies that will never be provoked
UiString.ConfigWindow_List_NoProvokeName: The name of the enemy that you don't want to provoke
UiString.HpAoe0Gcd: HP threshold for AoE healing oGCDs
UiString.HpAoeGcd: HP threshold for AoE healing GCDs
UiString.HpSingle0Gcd: HP threshold for single-target healing oGCDs
UiString.HpSingleGcd: HP threshold for single-target healing GCDs
UiString.ConfigWindow_Basic_AnimationLoc: The animation lock time for individual actions. For example, 0.6s.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Basic_ClickingDura: The clicking duration - RSR will try to click at this moment.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Auto_Description: Change how RSR automatically uses actions
UiString.ConfigWindow_Auto_HealingCondit: How RSR should use healing abilities
UiString.ConfigWindow_Auto_ActionConditi: This will change how RSR uses actions
UiString.ConfigWindow_Param_HostileDesc:
Enemy targeting logic. Adding more options cycles them when using /rotation Auto.
Use /rotation Settings TargetingTypes add <option> to add,
/rotation Settings TargetingTypes remove <option> to remove,
and /rotation Settings TargetingTypes removeall to remove all options.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Extra_Description: RSR focuses on the rotation itself. These are side features. Subject to removal at any time.
UiString.ConfigWindow_Events_Description: In this window, you can set which macro will be triggered after using an action.
UiString.ActionSequencer_NotDescription:
Click to make it reverse.
Is reversed: {0}

UiString.ConfigWindow_Condition_TargetWa: You should not use this, as this target isn't the action's target. Try selecting it from the action instead.

Config.Desc.0: Treat OmegaM/OmegaF as immune if you have their corresponding Packet Filter status (also applies to Normal).
Config.Desc.1: Treat Wolf of Wind/Wolf of Stone as immune if you don't have the corresponding status for it.
Config.Desc.2: This should clear up any targeting issues when a nail, the boss, and a flail are near each other at the same time.
Config.Desc.3: Treat Cells as immune if you don't have the corresponding status for it.
Config.Desc.4: Prioritize Doornail or Flail based on role and distance to target.
Config.Desc.5: Priotize Red Hot if you have firesnaking buff, and Deep Blue if you have watersnaking buff (also applies to Normal).
Config.Desc.6: Treat Bismark Shell/Bismark Corona as immune if you don't have the Whaleback status
Config.Desc.7: Treat Pall of Rage/Pall of Grief as immune if you don't have the corresponding status for it.
Config.Desc.8: Treat Shadow as immune if you don't have the corresponding status
Config.Desc.9: Treat Jagd Doll ads as immune when HP is less than 25%.
Config.Desc.10: Treat True Heart ad as immune.
Config.Desc.11: Treat OmegaM/OmegaF as immune if you have their corresponding Packet Filter status.
Config.Desc.12: Treat Crystal Of Darkness ad as immune.
Config.Desc.13: Treat bosses as immune if you don't have the corresponding status for it.
Config.Desc.14: Treat Colossus Rubricatus as immune while its casting scripted action which leads to its death.
Config.Desc.15: Treat Liars Lyre as immune if you don't have the Unfooled status.
Config.Desc.16: Treat Jailers in second boss fight as immune if you don't have corresponding buff.
Config.Desc.17: Treat Triton/Nereid/Phobos as immune if you don't have the corresponding status for it.
Config.Desc.18: Treat Eminent Grief as immune if you don't have Light Vengeance buff, and treat Devoured Eater as immune if you don't have Dark Vengeance buff.
Config.Desc.19: Treat Thanatos as immune if you don't have Astral Realignment buff.
Config.Desc.20: Treat Irminsul and Sawtooth as immune if you don't have corresponding buff.
Config.Desc.21: Treat each Superior Flight Unit as immune if you don't have corresponding buff.
Config.Desc.22: Treat each Hansel/Gretel as immune if you are at an angle that would cause you to take rebound damage from the shield mechanic.
Config.Desc.23: Treat each Ark Angel as immune if you don't have corresponding buff.
Config.Desc.24: Treat Alexander and/or Gordius Systems as immune if they have Perfect Defense.
Config.Desc.25: Treat Cloud of Darkness/Stygian as immune if you don't have corresponding buff.
Config.Desc.26: Custom logic to treat certain drakes as immune to kill them in a specific order for the purposes Variant path 12.
Config.Desc.27: Priotizes limited time mobs in treasure dungeons to resolve extra loot mechanic.
Config.Desc.28: Sets mobs with number plates as top priority in order from lowest to highest to resolve extra loot mechanic.
Config.Desc.29:
Full: Use all available AoE actions.
Cleave: Use only single-target AoE actions.
Off: Do not use any AoE actions.
Config.Desc.30: Never use any AoE action when this may attack mobs that are not hostile targets.
Config.Desc.31: Automatically turn off combat state when moving between different areas.
Config.Desc.32: Automatically turn off combat state during cutscenes.
Config.Desc.33: Automatically turn off combat state when you change your job/class.
Config.Desc.34: Automatically turn off combat state when your character dies.
Config.Desc.35: Automatically turn off combat state when a duty (instance) ends.
Config.Desc.36: Show a popup window with the changelog when the plugin updates.
Config.Desc.37: Display the plugin's current status in the server information bar.
Config.Desc.38: Show a toast notification with the combat state when changed.
Config.Desc.39: Prevents your character from moving while casting or using specific actions.
Config.Desc.40: This setting is only used if the rotation specifically supports automatic tincture usage.
Config.Desc.41: Enable to automatically use anti-knockback abilities when needed based on anti-knockback action list in List menu.
Config.Desc.42: Enable to allow the plugin to use HP potions automatically.
Config.Desc.43: Enable to allow the plugin to use MP potions automatically.
Config.Desc.44: Enable to allow the plugin to use Phoenix Down item. (Experimental feature)
Config.Desc.45: Enable to allow AoE actions to hit targets with priority markers.
Config.Desc.46: When teaching mode is active, automatically switch your target to match the rotation's suggested action target. Useful for tanks and healers where the optimal target may differ from your current selection.
Config.Desc.47: When teaching mode is active, display the rotation's suggested target name below the Next Action icon. Shown in orange if you don't have that target selected, green if you do.
Config.Desc.48: Displays rotating tips in the main panel; updates every 7 seconds.
Config.Desc.49: When enabled, actions you have disabled in RSR will be tinted on in-game hotbars.
Config.Desc.50: If you have another healer on the team, their healing might put the target player(s) above the healing threshold and you'll waste MP. This interrupts the cast if it happens.
Config.Desc.51: It is recommended to check this option if you are playing Raids or you can plan the heal and defense ability usage by yourself.
Config.Desc.52: When enabled and BossModReborn is loaded, RSR will use its timeline data to trigger defensive abilities before raidwides and tankbusters hit.
Config.Desc.53: Automatically use provoke when an enemy is attacking a non-tank member of the party while there is more than one tank in party.
Config.Desc.54:
1.    Self-Target Fallback:\r
If range is zero, always targets the player and returns all affectable targets at the player's position.\r
2.    Preferred Positions (OnLocations):\r
•    Tries to get predefined beneficial positions for the current territory.\r
•    If none are found and the content is a trial or raid, uses fallback points (e.g., 0,0 or 100,100 point as those are the center of arenas most of the time).\r
•    Picks the closest point to the player, applies a small random offset, and checks if it’s within effect range.\r
•    If so, returns that as the target area.\r
3.    Boss Positional Fallback:\r
•    If the current target is a boss with positional requirements and within range, uses the boss’s position (or a point within range) as the target area.\r
4.    Party Member Fallback:\r
•    Gathers party members within range + effect range.\r
•    Attempts to find a party member who is being attacked (tank or focus target).\r
•    If found, calculates whether to stay at the player’s position or move closer to the tank, based on distances and effect range.\r
•    If not found or not needed, defaults to the player’s position.
Config.Desc.55:
When enabled, non-healer jobs (such as DPS or tanks) will only use healing abilities if there are no healers in the party, or if all healers are incapacitated (at 0 HP). \r
If at least one healer is alive, non-healers will not use healing abilities.
Config.Desc.56: When using a Blue Mage Rotation, RSR can automatically set your spell book to the spells required by that rotation.
Config.Desc.57: If this is disabled, you will never use Swiftcast/Dualcast to raise players.
Config.Desc.58: Controls how much party members' HP must differ before using AoE healing instead of single-target heals. Lower values require party members to have more similar HP for AoE healing to trigger (more selective). Higher values allow AoE healing even when HP differences are larger (less selective). Adjust only if you want to fine-tune AoE heal behavior.
Config.Desc.61:
This setting controls how many oGCDs RSR will try to fit in a single GCD window
Lower numbers mean more oGCDs, but potentially more GCD clipping
Config.Desc.62: Do NOT use with XivAlexander, BMR tweaks enabled, or NoClippy - this should automatically disable itself if they are detected, but double check first!
Config.Desc.63: Configures the maximum simulated delay in milliseconds when using animation lock removal - this is required and cannot be reduced to zero. Setting this to 20ms will enable triple-weaving when using autorotation. The minimum setting to remove triple-weaving is 26ms. The minimum of 20ms has been accepted by FFLogs and should not cause issues with your logs.
Config.Desc.64: Dynamically adjusts cooldown and animation locks to ensure queued actions resolve immediately regardless of framerate limitations
Config.Desc.65: Connects to OverlayPlugin's WebSocket server and reacts to cactbot broadcast messages (raidwide, tankbuster, knockback, downtime/untargetable).
Config.Desc.66: Show a toast when a cactbot broadcast is received and mapped to a RotationSolver special.
Config.Desc.67: If enabled, movement actions target the object or mob at the center of your screen. If disabled, they target the object or mob your character is facing.
Config.Desc.68: Targets with attack markers will be prioritized for actions.
Config.Desc.69: Enemy parts, such as Titan's Heart, will be prioritized as targets.
Config.Desc.70: Targets with stop markers will not be attacked.
Config.Desc.71: Targets with only 1 HP will be treated as invincible and ignored; for rare cases where target is invincible but is not given a status for it.
Config.Desc.72: When in a Fate, only Fate targets are considered. When not in a Fate, Fate targets are ignored.
Config.Desc.73: If this is disabled, RSR will only use the game's built-in soft-targeting for allies for heals, shields, etc.
Config.Desc.74: (Do not set too low, can rip newly aggro'd dungeon mobs off tanks).
Config.Desc.75:
If the selection mode is based on character facing, i.e., targets within the character's viewpoint are moveable targets.
If the selection mode is screen-centered, i.e., targets within a sector drawn upward from the character's point are movable targets.
Cmd.RSCommands_OtherCommand.Failed_to_pa: Failed to parse the value as boolean.
Cmd.RSCommands_OtherCommand.Failed_to_fi: Failed to find the config in this rotation, please check it.
Cmd.RSCommands_OtherCommand.Removed_all_: Removed all TargetingTypes and reset to default list.
Tab.Actions.desc: Configure abilities and custom conditions for your current job.
Tab.Auto.desc: Configure general action usage and control settings.
Tab.AutoDuty.desc: Configure AutoDuty settings and view related information.
Tab.Debug.desc: Debug options for developers and rotation writers (disable when not in use).
Tab.List.desc: Configure reactive actions and status effect lists.
